mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% annualized (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate a tightening cycle amid persistent inflation, creating headwinds for equity markets and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS >4.5% and CPIAUCSL >3.0%, or interaction effect size < 0.02 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >55% of instances with UNRATE declines >0.5%, or t-test p >0.1 for negative returns in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential sales (RSAFS) indicate strong housing demand, boosting construction activity and corporate earnings, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >40% of instances with RSAFS increases >5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 in housing-driven periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak manufacturing activity, reducing corporate profits and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >50% of instances with INDPRO declines >2%, or t-test p >0.05 for negative returns in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and employment (PAYEMS) indicates robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >45% of instances with both GDPC1 >1.5% q/q and PAYEMS >100k, or interaction effect size < 0.03 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand for new construction, reducing related corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >50% of instances with HOUST declines >10%, or Rank IC < 0.02 in housing-driven periods.
